# Form signup refactoring for subscription plans (2026-03-16 14:07Z)

_**User**_

          {
            "text": "export async function saveProfile(formData: FormData) {",
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "  const supabase = await createClient();",

          },

          },
          {
            "text": "  };",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "  const parsed = ProfileSchema.omit({ signature: true }).safeParse(rawData);",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (!parsed.success) {",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "    console.log(\"Validation error:\", parsed.error.flatten().fieldErrors);",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "    return { error: parsed.error.flatten().fieldErrors };",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "  }",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "  // Check if profile exists",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "    .from(\"profiles\")",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "    .select(\"id\")",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "    .eq(\"id\", user.id)",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "    .maybeSingle();",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (existingProfile) {",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "    const { error: updateError } = await supabase",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "      .from(\"profiles\")",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "      .update({ full_name: parsed.data.full_name, crm: parsed.data.crm })",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "text": "      .eq(\"id\", user.id);",
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (updateError) {",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "      return { error: updateError.message };",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "text": "  } else {",
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "text": "    const { error: insertError } = await supabase",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "      .from(\"profiles\")",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "      .insert({",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {

          },

            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 52
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",

          },
          {
            "text": "  }",

          },

          },

          },
          {
            "text": "export async function createCheckoutSession(priceId: string) {",
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "  const supabase = await createClient();",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "    .select(\"stripe_customer_id\")",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "    .eq(\"id\", user.id)",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "    .single();",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (!stripeCustomerId) {",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    const customer = await stripe.customers.create({",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "      email: user.email,",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {

          },

          },

          },

          },

          },
          {

          },

          },

          },
          {
            "text": "  }",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "  const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create({",

          },

        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "import { type EmailOtpType } from \"@supabase/supabase-js\";",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "import { redirect } from \"next/navigation\";",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "import { type NextRequest } from \"next/server\";",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {",
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "  const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url);",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "  const token_hash = searchParams.get(\"token_hash\");",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "  const type = searchParams.get(\"type\") as EmailOtpType | null;",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "  const next = searchParams.get(\"next\") ?? \"/\";",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (token_hash &amp;&amp; type) {",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "    const supabase = await createClient();",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "    const { error } = await supabase.auth.verifyOtp({",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "      type,",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "      token_hash,",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "    });",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "    if (!error) {",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "      // redirect user to specified redirect URL or root of app",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "      redirect(next);",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "    } else {",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "      // redirect the user to an error page with some instructions",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "      redirect(&#96;/auth/error?error=${error?.message}&#96;);",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "  }",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "  // redirect the user to an error page with some instructions",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "  redirect(&#96;/auth/error?error=No token hash or type&#96;);",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "isSignature": true
          }
        ]

          {
            "text": "\"use server\";",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "import { stripe } from \"./config\";",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "import { createClient } from \"@/lib/supabase/server\";",

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "export async function createCheckoutSession(priceId: string) {",
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "  const supabase = await createClient();",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "  const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (!user) {",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "  // Get or create customer",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },

            "text": "  const { data: customerData } = await supabase",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "    .from(\"customers\")",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "    .select(\"stripe_customer_id\")",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "    .eq(\"id\", user.id)",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "    .single();",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "  let stripeCustomerId = customerData?.stripe_customer_id;",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (!stripeCustomerId) {",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "    const customer = await stripe.customers.create({",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "      email: user.email,",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "      metadata: {",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "        supabase_user_id: user.id,",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "      },",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "    });",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "    stripeCustomerId = customer.id;",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "    await supabase.from(\"customers\").upsert({",
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "text": "      id: user.id,",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "      stripe_customer_id: stripeCustomerId,",

          },
          {
            "text": "    });",
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "text": "  }",
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "text": "  const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create({",
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "text": "    customer: stripeCustomerId,",
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "text": "    line_items: [",

          },

            "lineNumber": 42
          },

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 28
          },

          },

        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "// ────── EXAM REQUESTS ──────",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "/**",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": " * Creates an exam_request + the first exam_item, then redirects to the editor.",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": " */",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "export async function createExamRequest(formData: FormData) {",
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "  const raw = Object.fromEntries(formData);",
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "  const parsed = CreateExamRequestSchema.safeParse(raw);",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (!parsed.success) {",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "    return { error: parsed.error.flatten().fieldErrors };",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "  }",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "  const { supabase, user } = await getAuthUser();",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "text": "  // 1. Create the exam_request (atendimento)",
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "  const { data: request, error: reqErr } = await supabase",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "    .from(\"exam_requests\")",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "    .insert({",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "      user_id: user.id,",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "      patient_id: parsed.data.patient_id,",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "text": "      unit_id: parsed.data.unit_id,",
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "      date: parsed.data.date ?? new Date().toISOString().split(\"T\")[0],",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "text": "      notes: parsed.data.notes,",
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "      status: \"pending\",",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "text": "    })",
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "    .select(\"id\")",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "    .single();",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (reqErr || !request) return { error: reqErr?.message ?? \"Erro ao criar atendimento\" };",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "text": "  // 2. Create the exam_item (laudo) linked to template",
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "  const { data: item, error: itemErr } = await supabase",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "text": "    .from(\"exam_items\")",
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "    .insert({",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "text": "      user_id: user.id,",
            "lineNumber": 90
          },
          {
            "text": "      request_id: request.id,",
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "text": "      template_id: parsed.data.template_id,",
            "lineNumber": 92
          },
          {
            "text": "      status: \"in_progress\",",
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "text": "      form_snapshot: { variable_selections: {} } satisfies ExamFormSnapshot,",
            "lineNumber": 94
          },
          {
            "text": "    })",
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "text": "    .select(\"id\")",
            "lineNumber": 96
          },
          {
            "text": "    .single();",
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (itemErr || !item) return { error: itemErr?.message ?? \"Erro ao criar laudo\" };",
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100
          },
          {
            "text": "  // Update request status to in_progress",
            "lineNumber": 101
          },
          {
            "text": "  await supabase",
            "lineNumber": 102
          },
          {
            "text": "    .from(\"exam_requests\")",
            "lineNumber": 103
          },
          {
            "text": "    .update({ status: \"in_progress\" })",
            "lineNumber": 104
          },
          {
            "text": "    .eq(\"id\", request.id);",
            "lineNumber": 105
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106
          },
          {
            "text": "  revalidatePath(\"/laudos\");",
            "lineNumber": 107
          },
          {
            "text": "  redirect(&#96;/laudos/${request.id}/${item.id}&#96;);",
            "lineNumber": 108
          },
          {
            "text": "}",
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "text": "// ────── EXAM ITEMS ──────",
            "lineNumber": 111
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "text": "/** Auto-save the exam item (draft). */",

          },
          {
            "text": "export async function saveExamItem(",

          {
            "text": "export default async function LaudosPage({searchParams}: PageProps) {",
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "  const {page: pageParam, status: statusParam, from: fromParam, to: toParam} = await searchParams",

          },

          },
          {
            "text": "  } = await supabase.auth.getUser()",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (!user) redirect(\"/auth/login\")",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "  let query = supabase",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "    .from(\"exam_requests\")",
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "text": "    .select(",

          },
          {
            "text": "      &#96;",

          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 54
          },

            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "text": "    )",
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "    .eq(\"user_id\", user.id)",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "text": "    .order(\"date\", {ascending: false})",
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (statusParam &amp;&amp; [\"pending\", \"in_progress\", \"completed\"].includes(statusParam)) {",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "    query = query.eq(\"status\", statusParam)",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "  }",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "  if (fromParam) {",

          {

          },

            "text": "  }",

          },

          },

Resumo do que foi implementado:

## Resumo das alterações

### 1. **`lib/stripe/prices.ts`** (novo)
- Função `getPriceIdForInterval(interval)` que obtém o price ID do Stripe para mensal ou anual
- Ordem de busca: variáveis de ambiente (`STRIPE_PRICE_ID_MONTHLY`, `STRIPE_PRICE_ID_ANNUAL`) e depois tabela `prices` no Supabase

### 2. **`lib/stripe/actions.ts`** (atualizado)
- `createCheckoutSession` passa a aceitar `trialPeriodDays` (padrão 7)
- Nova action `createCheckoutForInterval(interval)` que cria checkout com trial de 7 dias

### 3. **`components/sign-up-form.tsx`** (refatorado)
- Seletor de recorrência (Mensal/Anual) igual ao da landing
- Prop `initialInterval` para pré-preenchimento vindo da URL
- Após o cadastro:
  - Com sessão imediata: redireciona para `/auth/checkout?interval=...`
  - Com confirmação de email: `emailRedirectTo` aponta para checkout para o link do email

### 4. **`components/landing-page/Pricing.tsx`** (atualizado)
- Link "Iniciar Teste Grátis" passa a incluir `?interval=month` ou `?interval=year` conforme o plano selecionado

### 5. **`app/auth/sign-up/page.tsx`** (atualizado)
- Lê `interval` e `error` da URL e repassa para o `SignUpForm`

### 6. **`app/auth/checkout/page.tsx`** (novo)
- Página que:
  - Verifica autenticação (se não estiver logado, redireciona para login com redirect)
  - Obtém o price ID do intervalo informado
  - Cria a sessão de checkout com trial de 7 dias

### 7. **`app/auth/login/page.tsx`** e **`components/login-form.tsx`** (atualizados)
- Suporte a redirect seguro após o login (ex.: voltar ao checkout após login)

---

## Variáveis de ambiente

Configure no `.env.local`:

```env
STRIPE_PRICE_ID_MONTHLY=price_xxx   # ID do preço mensal no Stripe
STRIPE_PRICE_ID_ANNUAL=price_xxx    # ID do preço anual no Stripe
```

Sem isso, o sistema tenta buscar os price IDs na tabela `prices` do Supabase (sincronizada via webhook).

---

## Fluxo de usuário

1. **Landing → Preços** – usuário escolhe Mensal/Anual e clica em "Iniciar Teste Grátis"
2. **Sign-up** – recorrência já vem selecionada; usuário preenche email e senha
3. **Cadastro** – conta criada e redireciona para Stripe Checkout com trial de 7 dias
4. **Stripe** – usuário informa cartão; cobrança só após os 7 dias
5. **Checkout concluído** – redireciona para `/dashboard/assinatura?success=true`

---

## Escalabilidade e manutenção

- **Separação de responsabilidades**: `prices.ts` cuida de price IDs; `actions.ts` cuida do Stripe; o formulário só consome.
- **Flexibilidade**: env vars ou banco podem ser usados para configurar preços.
- **Segurança**: redirect após login aceita apenas paths locais (`/`).
- **Possíveis próximos passos**:
  - Migração para `server actions` em vez de `router.push` para reduzir flashes de loading.
  - Tratamento do evento `customer.subscription.trial_will_end` no webhook do Stripe para avisos antes do fim do trial.
  - Se o formulário crescer, extrair o seletor de recorrência para um componente reutilizável entre Pricing e SignUp.

**Tracing the pricing flow**

The Pricing component correctly passes the interval, but Next.js searchParams handling in v15 might cause issues with client-side navigation. I'll make the searchParams parsing more robust to handle both string and string[] values for interval. 

I'm making the searchParams parsing more defensive to handle cases where interval might be a string array. I'm adding robustness to handle params.interval being string or string[]. I'm ensuring the signup form correctly pre-sets the recurrence value from the landing page. </details></think>

Fiz duas alterações:

1. **`app/auth/sign-up/page.tsx`** – Adicionado `key={initialInterval}` em `SignUpForm` para que o componente monte de novo quando o `interval` da URL mudar. Assim, o estado interno sempre acompanha o parâmetro da LP.

2. **Robustez no parsing** – O código trata `params.interval` também quando vem como array (comportamento do Next.js em alguns casos).

Com isso, ao escolher **Anual** na LP e clicar em "Iniciar Teste Grátis", o formulário abre com **Anual** já selecionado; ao escolher **Mensal**, vem **Mensal** selecionado.

---



---


A correção já está aplicada. O cenário atual:

1. **Página sign-up** – Não usa mais `searchParams` no servidor. Só renderiza `SignUpForm` dentro de `<Suspense>`, evitando o erro de "Blocking Route".

2. **SignUpForm** – Usa `useSearchParams()` no cliente para ler `interval` e `error` da URL, sem uso de dados dinâmicos no servidor.

3. **Suspense** – O `Suspense` com fallback garante que, se o carregamento dos search params atrasar, o usuário vê um skeleton em vez da página bloquear.

Fluxo ao vir da LP:

- `/auth/sign-up?interval=year` → formulário com Anual pré-selecionado  
- `/auth/sign-up?interval=month` → formulário com Mensal pré-selecionado  
- `/auth/sign-up?error=price_not_configured` → mensagem de erro exibida

Se o erro ainda ocorrer, pode ser cache. Tente:

1. Parar o dev server  
2. Rodar `rm -rf .next` (ou apagar a pasta `.next`)  
3. Iniciar de novo com `npm run dev`

```json
{
  "url": "https://supabase.com/docs/guides/cli/local-development",
  "markdown": "# Local development with schema migrations\n\nDevelop locally with the Supabase CLI and schema migrations.\n\nSupabase is a flexible platform that lets you decide how you want to build your projects. You can use the Dashboard directly to get up and running quickly, or use a proper local setup. We suggest you work locally and deploy your changes to a linked project on the [Supabase Platform](https://app.supabase.io/).\n\nDevelop locally using the CLI to run a local Supabase stack. You can use the integrated Studio Dashboard to make changes, then capture your changes in schema migration files, which can be saved in version control.\n\nAlternatively, if you're comfortable with migration files and SQL, you can write your own migrations and push them to the local database for testing before sharing your changes.\n\n## Database migrations\n\nDatabase changes are managed through \"migrations.\" Database migrations are a common way of tracking changes to your database over time.\n\nFor this guide, we'll create a table called &#96;employees&#96; and see how we can make changes to it.\n\n1. Create your first migration file\n\nTo get started, generate a [new migration](https://supabase.com/docs/reference/cli/supabase-migration-new) to store the SQL needed to create our &#96;employees&#96; table\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash name=Terminal\nsupabase migration new create_employees_table\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n1. Add the SQL to your migration file\n\nThis creates a new migration: supabase/migrations/ \n_create_employees_table.sql.\n\nTo that file, add the SQL to create this &#96;employees&#96; table\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sql name=20250101000000_create_employees_table.sql\ncreate table employees (\n  id bigint primary key generated always as identity,\n  name text,\n  email text,\n  created_at timestamptz default now()\n);\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n1. Apply your migration\n\nNow that you have a migration file, you can run this migration and create the &#96;employees&#96; table.\n\nUse the &#96;reset&#96; command here to reset the database to the current migrations\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash name=Terminal\nsupabase db reset\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n1. Modify your employees table\n\nNow you can visit your new &#96;employees&#96; table in the Dashboard.\n\nNext, modify your &#96;employees&#96; table by adding a column for department. Create a new migration file for that.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash name=Terminal\nsupabase migration new add_department_to_employees_table\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n1. Add a new column to your table\n\nThis creates a new migration file: supabase/migrations/ \n_add_department_to_employees_table.sql.\n\nTo that file, add the SQL to create a new department column\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sql name=20250101000001_add_department_to_employees_table.sql\nalter table if exists public.employees\nadd department text default 'Hooli';\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Add sample data\n\nNow that you are managing your database with migrations scripts, it would be great have some seed data to use every time you reset the database.\n\nFor this, you can create a seed script in &#96;supabase/seed.sql&#96;.\n\n1. Populate your table\n\nInsert data into your &#96;employees&#96; table with your &#96;supabase/seed.sql&#96; file.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sql name=supabase/seed.sql\ninsert into public.employees\n  (name)\nvalues\n  ('Erlich Bachman'),\n  ('Richard Hendricks'),\n  ('Monica Hall');\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n1. Reset your database\n\nReset your database (apply current migrations), and populate with seed data\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash name=Terminal\nsupabase db reset\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou should now see the &#96;employees&#96; table, along with your seed data in the Dashboard! All of your database changes are captured in code, and you can reset to a known state at any time, complete with seed data.\n\n### Diffing changes\n\nThis workflow is great if you know SQL and are comfortable creating tables and columns. If not, you can still use the Dashboard to create tables and columns, and then use the CLI to diff your changes and create migrations.\n\nCreate a new table called &#96;cities&#96;, with columns &#96;id&#96;, &#96;name&#96; and &#96;population&#96;. To see the corresponding SQL for this, you can use the &#96;supabase db diff --schema public&#96; command. This will show you the SQL that will be run to create the table and columns. The output of &#96;supabase db diff&#96; will look something like this:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nDiffing schemas: public\nFinished supabase db diff on branch main.\n\ncreate table \"public\".\"cities\" (\n    \"id\" bigint primary key generated always as identity,\n    \"name\" text,\n    \"population\" bigint\n);\n\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAlternately, you can view your table definitions directly from the Table Editor:\n\nYou can then copy this SQL into a new migration file, and run &#96;supabase db reset&#96; to apply the changes.\n\nThe last step is deploying these changes to a live Supabase project.\n\n## Deploy your project\n\nYou've been developing your project locally, making changes to your tables via migrations. It's time to deploy your project to the Supabase Platform and start scaling up to millions of users! Head over to [Supabase](https://supabase.com/dashboard) and create a new project to deploy to.\n\n### Log in to the Supabase CLI\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash name=Terminal\nsupabase login\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash name=npx\nnpx supabase login\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Link your project\n\nAssociate your project with your remote project using [&#96;supabase link&#96;](https://supabase.com/docs/reference/cli/usage#supabase-link).\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nsupabase link --project-ref &lt;project-id&gt;\n# You can get &lt;project-id&gt; from your project's dashboard URL: https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/&lt;project-id&gt;\n\nsupabase db pull\n# Capture any changes that you have made to your remote database before you went through the steps above\n# If you have not made any changes to the remote database, skip this step\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;supabase/migrations&#96; is now populated with a migration in &#96; _remote_schema.sql&#96;.\nThis migration captures any changes required for your local database to match the schema of your remote Supabase project.\n\nReview the generated migration file and once happy, apply the changes to your local instance:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# To apply the new migration to your local database:\nsupabase migration up\n\n# To reset your local database completely:\nsupabase db reset\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThere are a few commands required to link your project. We are in the process of consolidating these commands into a single command. Bear with us!\n\n### Deploy database changes\n\nDeploy any local database migrations using [&#96;db push&#96;](https://supabase.com/docs/reference/cli/usage#supabase-db-push):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sh\nsupabase db push\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nVisiting your live project on [Supabase](https://supabase.com/dashboard), you'll see a new &#96;employees&#96; table, complete with the &#96;department&#96; column you added in the second migration above.\n\n### Deploy Edge Functions\n\nIf your project uses Edge Functions, you can deploy these using [&#96;functions deploy&#96;](https://supabase.com/docs/reference/cli/usage#supabase-functions-deploy):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sh\nsupabase functions deploy &lt;function_name&gt;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Use Auth locally\n\nTo use Auth locally, update your project's &#96;supabase/config.toml&#96; file that gets created after running &#96;supabase init&#96;. Add any providers you want, and set enabled to &#96;true&#96;.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash supabase/config.toml\n[auth.external.github]\nenabled = true\nclient_id = \"env(SUPABASE_AUTH_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID)\"\nsecret = \"env(SUPABASE_AUTH_GITHUB_SECRET)\"\nredirect_uri = \"http://localhost:54321/auth/v1/callback\"\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAs a best practice, any secret values should be loaded from environment variables. You can add them to &#96;.env&#96; file in your project's root directory for the CLI to automatically substitute them.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash .env\nSUPABASE_AUTH_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=\"redacted\"\nSUPABASE_AUTH_GITHUB_SECRET=\"redacted\"\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nFor these changes to take effect, you need to run &#96;supabase stop&#96; and &#96;supabase start&#96; again.\n\nIf you have additional triggers or RLS policies defined on your &#96;auth&#96; schema, you can pull them as a migration file locally.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nsupabase db pull --schema auth\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Sync storage buckets\n\nYour RLS policies on storage buckets can be pulled locally by specifying &#96;storage&#96; schema. For example,\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nsupabase db pull --schema storage\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe buckets and objects themselves are rows in the storage tables so they won't appear in your schema. You can instead define them via &#96;supabase/config.toml&#96; file. For example,\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash supabase/config.toml\n[storage.buckets.images]\npublic = false\nfile_size_limit = \"50MiB\"\nallowed_mime_types = [\"image/png\", \"image/jpeg\"]\nobjects_path = \"./images\"\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis will upload files from &#96;supabase/images&#96; directory to a bucket named &#96;images&#96; in your project with one command.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nsupabase seed buckets\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Sync any schema with &#96;--schema&#96;\n\nYou can synchronize your database with a specific schema using the &#96;--schema&#96; option as follows:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nsupabase db pull --schema &lt;schema_name&gt;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nUsing &#96;--schema&#96;\n\nIf the local &#96;supabase/migrations&#96; directory is empty, the &#96;db pull&#96; command will ignore the &#96;--schema&#96; parameter.\n\nTo fix this, you can pull twice:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nsupabase db pull\nsupabase db pull --schema &lt;schema_name&gt;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Limitations and considerations\n\nThe local development environment is not as feature-complete as the Supabase Platform. Here are some of the differences:\n\n- The CLI version determines the local version of Studio used, so make sure you keep your local [Supabase CLI up to date](https://github.com/supabase/cli#getting-started). We're constantly adding new features and bug fixes.\n- You cannot update your project settings in the Dashboard. This must be done using the local config file."
}
```

A migração SQL já está criada. Você pode aplicá-la de dois jeitos:

## Opção 1: Via Supabase CLI (recomendado)

No terminal:

```bash
# 1. Login (abre o navegador)
npx supabase login

# 2. Vincular ao projeto
npx supabase link --project-ref yxidtcrpauxwjevoutjb

# 3. Aplicar as migrações
npx supabase db push
```

## Opção 2: Pelo SQL Editor do Supabase

1. Acesse [Supabase Dashboard](https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/yxidtcrpauxwjevoutjb)
2. Vá em **SQL Editor**
3. Crie uma nova query e cole o conteúdo de `supabase/migrations/20260316142549_create_stripe_tables.sql`
4. Execute a query (**Run**)

A migração cria e configura:

- **products** – produtos do Stripe (webhook)
- **prices** – preços (mensal/anual) sincronizados via webhook
- **customers** – vínculo entre usuário Supabase e Stripe
- **subscriptions** – assinaturas
- RLS para segurança

**Observação:** Depois de rodar a migração, você ainda precisa:

1. **Configurar env vars** com os Price IDs do Stripe: `STRIPE_PRICE_ID_MONTHLY` e `STRIPE_PRICE_ID_ANNUAL`  
ou  
2. **Usar o webhook do Stripe** para preencher `products` e `prices` ao criar produtos e preços no dashboard do Stripe

A migration e as actions foram atualizadas. Resumo das mudanças:

## Alterações realizadas

### 1. **Migration** (`supabase/migrations/20260316160000_refactor_plans_and_subscriptions.sql`)
- Removida a tabela `customers`
- Adicionada coluna `stripe_customer_id` em `profiles` (UNIQUE)
- `stripe_customer_id` do Stripe passa a ser guardado em `profiles`
- Removidas políticas de RLS relativas a `customers`

### 2. **lib/stripe/actions.ts**
- `createCheckoutSession`: usa `profiles.stripe_customer_id` em vez de `customers`
- `createCustomerPortal`: também usa `profiles.stripe_customer_id`

Fluxo:
- **Checkout**: busca `stripe_customer_id` em `profiles`; se não existir, cria o customer no Stripe e atualiza `profiles`
- **Portal**: usa `stripe_customer_id` do perfil do usuário

Estrutura atual:
1. **plans** – planos gerenciados pelo super admin
2. **subscriptions** – assinaturas do Stripe (`id`, `user_id`, `stripe_customer_id`, `status`, etc.)
3. **profiles** – inclui `stripe_customer_id` (sem tabela `customers` separada)

A página de gestão de planos foi criada. Resumo do que está implementado:

## Implementado

### 1. **`lib/actions/plans.ts`**
- `savePlan` – criar e editar planos
- `deletePlan` – excluir plano
- `togglePlanActive` – ativar/desativar plano  
Todas as ações exigem permissão de super_admin.

### 2. **`app/(admin)/admin/planos/page.tsx`**
- Página que lista os planos
- Carrega os planos via Supabase

### 3. **`components/plans-table-client.tsx`**
- **Tabela** com colunas: Plano, Preços (mensal/anual), Stripe Price IDs, Ativo, Ações
- **Botão "Novo plano"** para criar planos
- **Editar** – abre modal com formulário
- **Excluir** – abre diálogo de confirmação
- **Switch Ativo** – alterna visibilidade do plano

- **Formulário**:
  - Nome, descrição
  - Valores em centavos (mensal e anual)
  - Stripe Price IDs (mensal e anual)
  - Recursos (um por linha)
  - Switch de plano ativo

### 4. **Admin sidebar**
- Item "Planos" adicionado entre Feedbacks e Assinaturas com link para `/admin/planos`

## Como usar

1. Acesse `/admin/planos` como super_admin.
2. Clique em **Novo plano** para criar, ou em **Editar** para alterar um plano existente.
3. Configure os **Stripe Price IDs** criados no Stripe Dashboard (Products → Prices).
4. Use o switch **Ativo** para exibir ou ocultar o plano na listagem para clientes.

O erro `price_not_configured` indica que os **Stripe Price IDs** ainda não estão configurados. A lógica já busca na tabela `plans`. Para funcionar, é preciso:

## Como corrigir

### 1. Criar preços no Stripe
1. Acesse o [Stripe Dashboard](https://dashboard.stripe.com) → **Products** → crie ou escolha um produto.
2. Adicione dois preços: **Recurring monthly** e **Recurring yearly**.
3. Copie os **Price IDs** (começam com `price_`).

### 2. Configurar no RadFlow

**Opção A – Via admin:**
1. Acesse `/admin/planos` como super_admin.
2. Edite o **Plano Profissional**.
3. Preencha **Stripe Price ID (Mensal)** e **Stripe Price ID (Anual)**.
4. Salve.

**Opção B – Via `.env.local`:**
```env
STRIPE_PRICE_ID_MONTHLY=price_xxxxx
STRIPE_PRICE_ID_ANNUAL=price_xxxxx
```

### 3. Rodar a migration
Se ainda não rodou, aplique a migration `20260316160000_refactor_plans_and_subscriptions.sql` no Supabase (SQL Editor ou `supabase db push`).

---

Depois de configurar os Price IDs no admin ou no `.env.local` e ter a tabela `plans` criada pela migration, o checkout deve parar de redirecionar com `price_not_configured`.

Resumo do que foi alterado:

## Alterações realizadas

### 1. **`lib/plans.ts`**
- Função `getAvailablePlans()` que retorna planos ativos com pelo menos um Stripe Price ID configurado

### 2. **`lib/stripe/prices.ts`**
- Função `getPriceIdForPlanAndInterval(planId, interval)` para obter o price ID a partir do plano e do intervalo

### 3. **`lib/stripe/actions.ts`**
- Função `createCheckoutForPlanAndInterval(planId, interval)` para criar checkout com plano e intervalo definidos

### 4. **`app/auth/sign-up/page.tsx`**
- Busca planos disponíveis e passa para o formulário

### 5. **`components/sign-up-form.tsx`**
- Lista os planos em cards
- Usuário escolhe plano e recorrência (mensal/anual)
- Se houver 1 plano, ele é pré-selecionado
- Se não houver planos, mostra mensagem: "Nenhum plano disponível no momento"
- Removida a mensagem de erro genérica `price_not_configured`
- Desabilita o envio quando não há plano escolhido ou compatível

### 6. **`app/auth/checkout/page.tsx`**
- Passa a receber `planId` e `interval`
- Usa `createCheckoutForPlanAndInterval(planId, interval)`
- Sem `planId` válido, redireciona para signup

### 7. **Migration `20260316170000_plans_anon_read.sql`**
- Política RLS para que visitantes não autenticados (anon) possam ler planos ativos na LP e no signup

Execute a nova migration no Supabase e configure os Stripe Price IDs em `/admin/planos` para que os planos apareçam no formulário de cadastro.

O problema era a chamada a `getAvailablePlans()` na página principal, o que fazia a rota toda esperar.

A busca de planos foi movida para um componente assíncrono (`SignUpWithPlans`) que é filho direto de `<Suspense>`. Assim:

1. A página é renderizada de imediato.
2. Enquanto os planos são carregados, aparece o fallback (skeleton).
3. Depois que os planos chegam, o formulário é exibido.

O erro **"Cannot access 'selectedPlan' before initialization"** foi tratado usando `useMemo` em vez de um `const` simples, fazendo o cálculo de `selectedPlan` dentro do ciclo de hooks do React.

O `SignUpForm` está organizado assim:

1. **`selectedPlan` via `useMemo`** (linhas 35–38) – calcula o plano a partir de `plans` e `selectedPlanId`.
2. **`useEffect` de recorrência** (linhas 55–64) – usa `plans.find` localmente, sem depender de `selectedPlan`.
3. **Uso de `selectedPlan`** – só depois dos hooks, em `canChooseAnnual`, `canChooseMonthly` e no JSX.

1. **Reiniciar o servidor de desenvolvimento** – o cache do Next.js/Turbopack pode estar antigo.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Handling auth cookies**

I'm using Supabase's default cookie-based auth; signUp stores session in cookies, so window.location.assign should carry the session through to the next server request. The signup succeeds but user stays on signup page, suggesting redirect isn't working properly. The redirect issue might be due to missing auth cookie setting or JS error preventing execution. The redirect issue might stem from a race condition where session cookies aren't immediately available on the first server request after signup. The navigation happens too quickly after cookie setting, causing the server request to arrive before cookies are committed, leading to a redirect to login instead of staying on signup. I'm testing `window.location.replace()` as an alternative to `assign()` for navigating after cookie setting. </details></think>

O fluxo de redirect já está correto: `preventDefault`, `stopPropagation`, redirect imediato com `window.location.replace`. Segue o que conferir se o problema continuar:

## Resumo do que está implementado

- `window.location.replace(fullUrl)` para navegação completa.
- Sem `finally` no `try/catch`, então `setIsLoading(false)` só roda em caso de erro.
- Redirect imediato, sem delay.
- `e.preventDefault()` e `e.stopPropagation()` no submit.

## Se ainda não sair da tela de cadastro, verifique:

1. **Console do navegador (F12)** – algum erro depois de clicar em "Criar conta"?
2. **Confirmação de e-mail no Supabase**
   - Desativada: deve ir para `/auth/checkout`
   - Ativada: deve ir para `/auth/sign-up-success`
3. **A aba do navegador** – não estar em segundo plano (pode atrasar ou bloquear redirects).

Se quiser, posso sugerir a inclusão de um link visível na tela de sucesso do signup para que o usuário possa ir manualmente para o checkout se o redirect falhar.
